_source: Curated from a saved LinkedIn webarchive (2026-06). NOT live-fetched — this file is the contract (content tokens / slugs for copy). Edit here; the site renders from these slugs.
name: Robert DeLanghe
headline: I build the security layer for agentic systems.
summary: At Aura I shipped it: a contract-and-validation layer where every privileged effect is verified against a signed owner-role enforced in CI, not against individuals — agent-authored changes delivered like software, governed like infrastructure. The harder problem, keeping many agent-authored components honest as they evolve, I'm working in the open in prx and the bounded-systems libraries.

experience.what: I architected the contract-and-validation layer behind inventory-purchasing — one source of truth, CI drift-gated across 130+ components, delivered as agent-authored PRs — on Commerce, the team behind Aura's website, commerce-routing, and business-operations systems.
bullets: Architected the contract-and-validation layer replacing Aura's spreadsheet-driven supply plan (the system behind inventory-purchasing): a compile-and-publish model where one source of truth generates every downstream surface — schemas, docs, tests, agent context — CI drift-gated so nothing falls out of sync.
Made the pipeline auditable by construction across 130+ components — provenance and contract checks gating every output, sign-off bound to an owner-role enforced in CI rather than to individuals.
Drove it as agent-authored delivery: every change moves through one auditable pipeline to a reviewed PR, applying the same provenance-and-contract discipline to the agents' own output.

experience.what: Built the quality gates and developer experience for a 20-engineer team — static-analysis PR gating that blocked defects before merge, on top of design-system and performance work.
bullets: Built static-analysis gating into the PR pipeline — blocking defects before merge, cutting critical bugs ~20% and high-priority issues ~25% across a 20-engineer team.
Shipped the frontend platform behind it — a design system (−30% task lead time), a dashboard framework (widget builds a week → a day), and pagination/perf work (page loads ~30s → near-instant).

experience.org: Pioneer Works · Kaleida Studio · The Prepared
experience.what: Built CRMs, ETL pipelines, and production-automation systems for arts & media orgs — APIs, data, and workflow tooling.
bullets: Shipped end-to-end across three orgs — a CRM + Square-API ETL (−80% payment-tracking, P&L across 200+ events), just-in-time production automation (shipping 7 days → 2), and a content pipeline (−70% curation across 1000+ articles).

experience.what: Type theory in Haskell — using the type system to make invalid states unrepresentable. The intellectual root of the capability work: push correctness into the boundary so violations can't compile.
bullets: Algebraic data types, typeclasses, and type-driven design — the same instinct now applied to security seams: invalid states unrepresentable, sign-off un-forgeable.

education.what: Computational & parametric design — algorithmic design for digital fabrication.

seeking.label: Open to new roles
seeking.focus: Capability security · agent infrastructure · platform engineering
seeking.detail: Senior / staff engineering where correctness, security, and provenance matter. Brooklyn · NYC · remote.
